notes: Batons are generally considered weapons in the UAE and cannot be carried by civilians without authorisation.
Kubotans may be treated as ordinary items but weaponised versions can be considered illegal weapons
Self-defence keychains may be legal if they are ordinary items, but weaponised designs can be treated as illegal weapons.
Carrying a sword in public is generally restricted in the UAE unless it is for ceremonial or cultural purposes.
Tasers are restricted weapons in the UAE and are not legal for civilian ownership.
Pepper spray is generally not legal for civilian possession in the UAE
Switchblades are prohibited in the UAE and cannot be legally owned or carried.
Butterfly knives are illegal in the UAE and are treated as prohibited weapons.
Stun guns are prohibited for civilian use in the UAE and are considered illegal weapons.
Brass knuckles are illegal in the UAE and are treated as prohibited weapons.
